payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You have until tomorrow to try it out, so if you're like, "I don't want to pay for Alpha!" Well, you don't have to for 60 days now! Just put SHIPMATES in, and you'll be fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's so good!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's genuinely one of the coolest experiences I've ever had as a player in a game.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we invented a new form of theater; it's weird.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you have the opportunity, it's on Alpha. If you have Alpha, go check it out. If you don't have Alpha, there's a 60-day free trial code. What's the code?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: SHIPMATES.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: SHIPMATES.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Only today, though. Today's the last day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Until tomorrow.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The throbbing pain, and you're not able to focus in this eye at the time. You're effectively unable to see out of this eye really well until your next long rest. It's pretty damaged just from the brief grabbing. You could try to heal him, if you want. Your mind comes back to you for a minute. You're like, "What the fuck is wrong with me?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As his fingers are digging into the socket and the other eye's just staring at this piercing circular gaze in his grasp in his right. You see the blood beginning to trail down his face in two different places. He's gotten his fingers partway behind his own eye. You rush up and grab him by the wrist, put your other hand right at the front of his face, pushing his nose and cheek up. You pull it back and you watch as, from behind the eyelid, his fingers slip out. He's fighting it with all his might. As you pull back, as soon as you get it free, with your other hand, you backhand. You knock the eye out of his other hand, and it goes (falling noise) and disappears beneath the mist.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we see where it landed?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to follow the eye.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do not touch it, Percy!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I see?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: The throbbing pain, and you're not able to focus in this eye at the time. You're effectively unable to see out of this eye really well until your next long rest. It's pretty damaged just from the brief grabbing. You could try to heal him, if you want. Your mind comes back to you for a minute. You're like, "What the fuck is wrong with me?!"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As his fingers are digging into the socket and the other eye's just staring at this piercing circular gaze in his grasp in his right. You see the blood beginning to trail down his face in two different places. He's gotten his fingers partway behind his own eye. You rush up and grab him by the wrist, put your other hand right at the front of his face, pushing his nose and cheek up. You pull it back and you watch as, from behind the eyelid, his fingers slip out. He's fighting it with all his might. As you pull back, as soon as you get it free, with your other hand, you backhand. You knock the eye out of his other hand, and it goes (falling noise) and disappears beneath the mist.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we see where it landed?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm going to follow the eye.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do not touch it, Percy!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I see?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. Over the next three or four minutes, as you guys pat through and tear roots apart and sections of the forest floor, there is no sign of where the eye landed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (louder) Can you make the mist go away?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can you try?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I half-heartedly do a Gust cantrip while still sitting next to Vex.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as some of the mist dissipates and very quickly begins to roll back. The mist here is almost aggressive in its need to fill the space. Like a lot of things in the Feywild, it almost has its own sense of nature and purpose. It's not going to let someone push itself back if it wishes to exist on the forest floor. As you're rushing in to help, because you've already made the investigation check and the mist is rolling in, and based on his inability to find it, you're not even quite sure where it landed. Make your own investigation check, but you're at disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Hey, that's high! That's not. Three.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You go over and grab your holy symbol and kneel to the ground, focus on whatever this dark relic is that seems to have plagued you in two different ways in the past ten minutes. As you concentrate, you feel the divine light of Sarenrae come and restore warmth from the center of this cold forest. You feel the energy flow through you and the image of her face descending from above, her mouth opening and she exhales this breath over you, and as it does, you suddenly can clarify your visual perception on this object. You know exactly the direction it is. You start walking in that direction. You push past Grog and push past Percival. You find a portion of the ground and you look, and it's not there, but you know it's that direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Hey, that's high! That's not. Three.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Over the next three or four minutes, as you guys pat through and tear roots apart and sections of the forest floor, there is no sign of where the eye landed.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: If I were to cast Locate Object, would that work?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can certainly try.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay!

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It can range based on the purity of the diamond, how well it's been cut. You can make an intelligence check, if you'd like.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe our werewolf friends are still around.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I was going to say I have a terrible idea. I think they wanted to leave, so what if they know of some hidden treasure trove around here? That could, you know, tit for tit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I might be able to buy diamonds, as well. If we go to a city.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If we rest, I may be able to make them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 25,000 gold worth of diamonds.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How big is that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It could be a lot of smaller diamonds.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: But how big? How many?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'll pull out one diamond that I have that's worth 1,500 gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I feel within a tinker theory is how figuring out how much diamonds--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We would need 20 more of these.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You see a number of tracks that would resemble creatures of that size or larger. Some seem bear-like, some seem larger than that, though similar claws and heavy footprints. Some that are smaller and skittery. You see there was a small cluster of what might be deer-like creatures, but you're not quite sure what the tracks are. There's plenty of life that wanders through here. Apparently, since you've arrived, none that seem bold enough to approach you too closely in this clustered clearing you're in. Can't even call it a clearing, because you maybe have a total of ten feet you're all crammed into at this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No! The eye! I can get rid of the eye!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No, it makes me nervous, Scanlan. I want to keep our... eyes on it. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Fuck off!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe we should vote on this. Should we give it to me? Who says aye?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can buy my vote.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't have that much.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you do. You can buy my vote.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't have enough.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are there any animals around?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a nature check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're familiar with forests, definitely, and you can see telltale signs of scurrying, telltale signs of track marks in the ground, feces left behind by living creatures. You do see signs of things that live throughout these forests everywhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Anything that looks like it would be the size of an adult deer or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Doe, a deer? A female deer?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm thinking a male deer. Yes, a stag. A white stag. Any stag, really, just an animal that's big.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: That's pretty sad. All right. The rest of you find yourselves to a night's rest, seemingly protected for the time being by the surrounding Shademirk Forest, no longer the Shademirk Bog. And unconsciousness takes you, Grog taking first watch. Now, I'm going to ask you guys, if you wouldn't mind, to step into the kitchen.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Small miracles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They like us now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You guys crash; I'll take first watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to pull the armor up so we can keep it. Did you want--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. We cuddle between the armor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's weird. I'm not getting into it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's the saddest thing.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Your resolve is admirable. Your love of your chosen family is vibrant. What I offer you is the power to see this conflict through to its end. You will be briefly beyond the very grasp of death, and I will hold back any attempt for you to transition. You will be a bulwark of my power in cold flesh, walking between heartbeats, unstoppable. You will have the power to walk once more with Vox Machina, with your sister, with your Keyleth. And should you use this power wisely, perhaps save Exandria, save them, save her. But when it's done and this foul, undying king lies dead or sealed, you will return to me, my champion, evermore." The imposing image now shifts and changes. In an instant, the giant being is now the simple woman, the mask you once saw removed now missing. And there, the somewhat sad, beautiful pale face of the Raven Queen as she steps forward. The silence is broken by the sounds of her footsteps across nothing. (footsteps) Her hand comes up. Warm. You weren't expecting, as it traces across your brow and curves across to your chin and pulls it up. "Do you accept my gift?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We have."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where is my sister?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "She lives, still. She is beyond my influence. However, your body is ash, and your soul is steeped in the divine energies that uphold the paths between life and death. You've earned your eternal rest." The mask begins to recede into the darkness.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. No! Come back. No! I will not leave them behind. I want to help them, still.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The faint gray of the mask that begins to fade into shadow stops and comes back even brighter, vibrant, almost. You sense the mystery of the Raven Queen shift from this entity that dodges and weaves to instead a pillar of power of life and death that is both awe-striking and awe-fearing. And for a moment, you realize how small you are in her presence. "You truly see, to seek this, to bring it to its end?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Whatever it takes.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: 19, okay. While there is no real change from day to night in the Feywild, it's in that perpetual nearly dusk, sunset, purple-orange skyline, which you can see elements of creeping through since you're higher up in the brush of the forest. You do hear the shifting of sounds. The evening owl hooting has changed to more gentle bird chirps and sounds that would usually befit birds that lead to morning to you. Whether that be the forest itself, its natural cycle, or it altering to fit your cycle of sleep. At the end of your watch-- you've had your full rest, but are still a little groggy, you hear what sounds like the crushing of leaves on the ground below you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So as you make your watch. Who's making second watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll take both.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need to rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't, boo.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you do. You need to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You don't want to be exhausted.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You've got to get your spells back, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How long does he have to rest so he's not exhausted?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: These are all long rests. We're all getting long rests. I'll take second watch.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm already exhausted. I frenzied last.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, so if you don't rest, you don't get rid of that point of exhaustion.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We've all learned what happens when I get two points of exhaustion. Good things.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Vecna comes back, and we all die.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Vecna is back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so Percival, second watch? Perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That'll do. That's a 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Your watch continues without issue. Final watch is?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'll take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pike. Final watch. Perception check.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: And with a singular howl, you all shift out of the Feywild and then rapidly appear right on the outskirts of the outer wall of Vasselheim. You see the large, almost Sumerian-looking structures and walls that surround the outside. The ancient architecture of the place definitely has the oldest look, and you remember seeing it from the top when you flew in through your first skyship arrival into Vasselheim. The snow-covered ground and snow-covered trees as part of the Vesper Timberland.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Also, the longer we're here, potentially, the more time gets wonky.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're on a ticking clock, here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go to Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I like this idea. It's a little crazy, but I like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We should start with one god and see how it goes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Start with one that really likes you. So Sarenrae's a good call there.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: We'll work our way through in a montage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Worked for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: A god-tage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do we hold hands?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we all get tattoos? (singing) We need a god-tage!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To Vasselheim.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we're just going to go?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we didn't talk to Artagan. Artagan, are you here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that how it works?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can you hear us? Do you want to help us?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Maybe if we do something even stupider than holding hands in a circle.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Did any werewolves show up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Right as we go, I start howling. (howling)

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Sheila begins to return two minutes later with acolyte Vincent Clor, who's this mousy-looking guy. His eyes are smaller than they should be it seems for the size of his round face, he comes up with a big toothy grin. "I was told that you were seeking my presence, wonderful Mistress Pike."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Holy shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's convenient.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Different area on the plane. The Fortress of the Sun.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (singing) Fortress of the Sun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The blessed fields of Elysium are an idyllic plane of good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are these things Pike knows because of her expertise, or does everyone in the world know a certain amount about?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They know the gods, and they know the realms, but the name of the plane? This is known to her because of her study of religion. This is more of an academic understanding of the real essence of the gods, where they come from.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I didn't think it was going to work! Sheila, I think--

payload.trigger_phrase: you make your way out of the temple
payload.raw_text: Yep. You're those friends. They smile and walk away, but Vincent Clor definitely seems a bit shaken by the knowledge that you just brought to him. But you make your way out of the temple. Where are you off to? What's your next direction?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Melora is really good at it, too. We should talk to Melora.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: Yeah, it's fine. We know people.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns_last5.text: Thank you. Keep up the good work. You're doing, just, all the great stuff.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: I like the idea that anywhere we go, we embarrass the one person that knows anybody else.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "You've been walking with him for 20 minutes."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You were with him as Tary!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I wasn't there! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The one in adorned armor. "Sir, might I have your name and purpose here in Vasselheim?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (high-pitched) Peter Frostbucket! I seek Lord of the Quad Roads!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (giggling) Make a deception check with disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't need it! Negative one! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. We're going to have to kill some guards, aren't we? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two and a one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They did that, not us!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Sarenrae comes down and says, never mind!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Your picture is going to be up in a small office by the gates.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The captain-looking individual goes, "Right, Mr. Frostbucket. We would like the both of you to please come with us." The two guards rush into your flank a bit and place their hands on your shoulder and your shoulder.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Me? I just met this man. I've never seen him before in my life.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: More than that. Like four hours or so.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And also, dear. The communication system that you and Percival and Tary set up. Is it here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Where's the closest?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The Fire Ashari. Why?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, we've got two things on our plate, right? We need to start--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Alerting the cities.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Maybe you can send a message. Do you have that ability?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To the Fire Ashari, and then to the fields of Elysium?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To the Fire Ashari, then to the fields of Elysium.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's mist there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Is it fast to mist to the Fire Ashari?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just I Dream of Jeannie us there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we're going to the Ashari? What happened with Sarenrae?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's next.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going to do that, we're just getting the word out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Misting to the Fire Ashari is not that long, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: In mist form, it won't take you but a few hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's what I thought. A couple of hours.

payload.raw_text: They don't hear you say it because this is so fast. You have no reason to have a conversation. There before you, you see the glimmering, almost burning lattice of the Divine Gate come streaking towards you at immense speed, and you know you've passed through comfortably once before, so you hope and brace for impact and pass through again, that warmth almost coming to a similar state of burning, but then leaving you behind. And just as fast, as it passes by you, you land, your feet hitting ground, ground that gives.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns_last5.text: No, this is not going to be dangerous, right? We're just going to talk to some friendly people.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns_last5.text: It's a good plane of existence.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: You Plane Shift? All right, as you all put your hands in each other's, you feel the familiar sense of your physical form being drawn through the barrier of the Prime Material Plane, and much like when you transitioned to the Nine Hells, you feel yourselves pulled into this temporary space, this darkness, this sea of endless void with a distant blinking of stars that shimmer. You understand this as being the Astral Sea.
